subject: English
chapter: The Case of the Fifth Word
title: Extended Reading Passages with Questions – The Case of the Fifth Word
instructions: Read each passage carefully. Answer the questions using information only from the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Encyclopedia Brown was a young boy known for his intelligence and excellent memory. He lived in the town of Idaville and was famous for solving mysteries that puzzled even adults.
questions: Who was Encyclopedia Brown?
Where did he live?
What was he known for?
Who was puzzled by the mysteries he solved?
Why was he famous in his town?

passages.paragraph: Encyclopedia Brown’s father was the Chief of Police in Idaville. At dinner, he often discussed difficult cases with his son because the boy had an extraordinary ability to analyze clues.
questions: Who was Encyclopedia Brown’s father?
What was his job?
When did he discuss cases with his son?
Why did he share the cases with Encyclopedia?
What special ability did Encyclopedia have?

passages.paragraph: One evening, Chief Brown mentioned a robbery that had taken place years earlier. Two masked men had stolen valuable jewellery from a shop, but the police had never found the stolen items.
questions: What did Chief Brown mention one evening?
What had the robbers stolen?
How many robbers were involved?
Did the police recover the jewellery?
When had the robbery happened?

passages.paragraph: Tim Nolan was one of the men suspected in the robbery. However, the police had never found enough evidence to prove his guilt.
questions: Who was suspected in the robbery?
Why was Nolan suspected?
Did the police have enough evidence?
What could the police not prove?
What problem did the police face in the investigation?

passages.paragraph: Nolan had a friend named Daniel Davenport, who was also suspected in the crime. The two men had met while they were in prison and became close friends.
questions: Who was Nolan’s friend?
What was Davenport suspected of?
Where did Nolan and Davenport meet?
What kind of relationship did they develop?
Why were they both suspected?

passages.paragraph: Years later, Nolan died suddenly after suffering a stroke. On the kitchen table, the police found his will and a strange message written on a piece of paper.
questions: What happened to Nolan?
What caused his death?
Where was the will found?
What else was found on the table?
Why was the message unusual?

passages.paragraph: The message contained four strange words: Nom, Utes, Sweden, and Hurts. The police could not understand their meaning and were confused by the unusual message.
questions: How many words were in the message?
What were the four words?
Why were the police confused?
What did the words seem like at first?
What problem did the police face?

passages.paragraph: When Chief Brown told his son about the message, Encyclopedia Brown immediately began to think carefully. He realized that the words were related to the days of the week.
questions: Who heard about the message from Chief Brown?
What did Encyclopedia Brown start doing?
What did he realize about the words?
What were the words connected to?
How did he approach the problem?

passages.paragraph: Encyclopedia Brown discovered that the four words represented Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday without the letters 'day'. This helped him find the missing fifth word.
questions: What did the four words represent?
Which days were mentioned?
What letters were removed from the words?
What did this discovery help him find?
Why was the pattern important?

passages.paragraph: The missing fifth word pointed to a fir tree in Nolan’s nursery. When the police searched under the tree, they finally found the stolen jewellery.
questions: What did the missing fifth word indicate?
Where was the tree located?
What did the police find under the tree?
Who solved the mystery?
How did the story end?
